Now UMN Student

Studying computer science while building research, data, and systems projects that connect theory with implementation.

Location
Minnesota, USA
Focus

About

A concise academic profile.

This page follows the structure of my CV, with the most important context surfaced first.

I am Shengming Zhou, a computer science student at the University of Minnesota. Before transferring to UMN, I studied Mathematics and Applied Mathematics at Shandong University.

My academic work centers on databases, mathematics, and research workflows. I enjoy building implementations that make abstract ideas measurable, testable, and useful in practice.

Background

Education and research experience.

These are the academic and research contexts that best explain what I am working on now.

University of Minnesota

Bachelor of Arts in Computer Science

2025 - Present

GPA: 4.0/4.0

Relevant coursework: Matrix Theory, Database System, Graphics.

Shandong University

Bachelor of Science in Mathematics and Applied Mathematics

2023 - 2025

GPA: 82.1/100

Relevant coursework: Real and Complex Analysis, Probability Theory, ODE.

Research Assistant

Long-horizon LLM Memory and Personalization Benchmark Project

2026

  • Built automated data processing and evaluation workflows in Python for large-scale benchmarking tasks.
  • Managed experiment environments on Linux servers, including dependency setup, debugging, and performance troubleshooting.
  • Developed validation scripts and quality checks to improve dataset consistency and output reliability.

Selected Work

Projects that reflect my current direction.

These two projects best represent the mix of theory, implementation, and evaluation I want to keep developing.

2025

Database Buffer Manager Replacement Policy

featured

A coursework implementation of a Time-Window Replacement policy for a database buffer manager.

C/C++ Database Systems Performance Evaluation

2025

Thermodynamic Formalism for Subshifts of Finite Type

featured

A research report connecting symbolic dynamics with entropy, invariant measures, fractal geometry, and constrained coding.

Dynamical Systems Perron-Frobenius Theory Numerical Verification

Notes

A small place for ongoing interests.

I do not need a full blog yet, but I do want a place to record what I am currently exploring.

2026-04-13

Current Interests

I am most interested in the intersection of mathematics, databases, and practical machine learning workflows.
